events.description: Board of Indian Commissioners Secretary Malcolm McDowell forwarded batch of replies regarding condition of patent-in-fee Indians to Commissioner of Indian Affairs

Board of Indian Commissioners issued Bulletin No. 142 on releasing Indians from Government supervision, including letters from superintendents and farmers of Oklahoma Indian jurisdictions outside Five Civilized Tribes
Letter sent to J.C. Hart requesting reports on effect upon Indians released from Government control
Second letter sent requesting reports from agency farmers on released Indians
J.C. Hart came to Pawnee Indian Agency as Superintendent in July 1919
J.C. Hart observed Sioux of South Dakota
J.C. Hart observed Oneidas of Wisconsin
Recent health survey at Ponca City showed large number of cases of syphilis in various stages
Moses Bellmard served as 1st Lieutenant in World War I in France
Wm. Louis Pappan was a soldier in France during WWI
Report of June 30, 1930 giving statistics on tribal allotments and acreage under Pawnee Agency
Nelson Thompson was in jail for drunkenness the week of the report
A Pappan family member got in jail for running a still in the fall
Keenan Pappan's relative (name unclear) arrested a few days before report was written

financial_transactions.description: Albert Primeaux sold patented land for approximately $600
Maggie LaDue sold most of her land except 80 acres for approximately $3000
Ben Told's wife still holds 120 acres in trust worth approximately $12,000
George Smith Jr. mortgaged his land for $5,000 or $6,500 with 5-year lease
Laura P. Randall's 340 surplus acres sold; 160 homestead deeded to white wife
Old dependent Indians receiving $100.00 monthly from Government are scarcely able to exist
Talbott Farnsworth sold 80 acres and deeded 30 acres to his child
Indians expressed preference for $10,000 in hand over promise of $100.00 next week, illustrating short-term thinking

financial_transactions.payee: Robert Tah, Railroad Cisco, David Williams
financial_transactions.description: Three Tonkawa Indians converted all their fee patent lands into money within the last three years
Ida B. Wifenbarger retained all 400 acres and white husband is buying more land

correspondence.recipient: Indian Service / Board of Indian Commissioners
correspondence.subject: Report on fee patent Indians under Tonkawa Sub-agency during eleven years in service
correspondence.outcome: Reported only one successful fee patent recipient (Lona Ciserneos), noted poor outcomes for Robert Tah, Railroad Cisco, and David Williams

legislative_actions.outcome: Kaw Treaty specifies that Kaw Indians must make written applications for competency certificates to have restrictions removed from homestead allotments
